zh.Dalkeith, , Midlothian, Scotland: 英國蘇格蘭米德洛迪安達爾凱斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當James Douglas 1st of Dalkeith於1325年出生在英國蘇格蘭米德洛迪安達爾凱斯時，他的父親Sir John Douglas of Lothian22歲，母親Agnes Graham - Heiress of Dalkeith17歲。他於大約1347年與Lady Agnes de Dunbar在英國蘇格蘭米德洛迪安達爾凱斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1420年在他的家鄉享年95歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭米德洛迪安達爾凱斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James Douglas 1st of Dalkeith于1325年出生在英国苏格兰米德洛迪安達爾凱斯时，他的父亲Sir John Douglas of Lothian22岁，母亲Agnes Graham - Heiress of Dalkeith17岁。他于大约1347年与Lady Agnes de Dunbar在英国苏格兰米德洛迪安達爾凱斯结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1420年在他的家乡享年95岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰米德洛迪安達爾凱斯。

nameMeanings.name: Douglas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of various places called from their situation on a river named with Gaelic dubh ‘dark, black’ + glas ‘stream’ (a derivative of glas ‘blue’). There are several localities in Scotland and Ireland so named, but the one from which the surname is derived in most if not all cases is Douglas in Lanarkshire 20 miles south of Glasgow, the original stronghold of the influential Douglas family and their retainers.

History: The family taking their name from Douglas in Lanarkshire were of Flemish origin. They rose to great prominence in the 14th and 15th centuries, controlling the earldoms of Douglas, Morton, and Angus, and later, Queensberry.

variants.name: Douglass
meanings: Scottish and English (Durham and Northumbria): variant of   Douglas  .

History: William Douglass, a physician recognized for his identification and description of an epidemic of scarlet fever, was born c. 1691 in Gifford, Haddington County, Scotland, and settled in Boston in 1718. The abolitionist, orator, and journalist Frederick Douglass assumed the name after escaping from slavery in 1838 and traveling to Massachusetts. Son of a white father and a slave with some Indian blood, he was born Frederick Augustus Washington Bailey c. 1817 in Tuckahoe, MD.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When James Douglas 1st of Dalkeith was born in 1325, in Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Sir John Douglas of Lothian, was 22 and his mother, Agnes Graham - Heiress of Dalkeith, was 17. He married Lady Agnes de Dunbar about 1347, in Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died in 1420, in his hometown, at the age of 95, and was buried in Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James Douglas 1st of Dalkeith est né en 1325, à Dalkeith, Midlothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John Douglas of Lothian, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Agnes Graham - Heiress of Dalkeith, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Lady Agnes de Dunbar environ 1347, à Dalkeith, Midlothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en 1420, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 95 ans, et a été enterré à Dalkeith, Midlothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: James Douglas 1st of Dalkeithが1325年にイギリススコットランドミドロスアンダルケイスで生まれた時，父Sir John Douglas of Lothianは22歳，母Agnes Graham - Heiress of Dalkeithは17歳であった。約1347年にイギリススコットランドミドロスアンダルケイスでLady Agnes de Dunbarと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1420年に故郷で95歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドミドロスアンダルケイスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: James Douglas 1st of Dalkeith(이)가 1325년 영국 스코틀랜드 미들로스이언 다케이스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Douglas of Lothian(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Graham - Heiress of Dalkeith(은)는 17세였다. 그는 대략 1347년 영국 스코틀랜드 미들로스이언 다케이스에서 Lady Agnes de Dunbar(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1420년 그의 고향에서 95세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 미들로스이언 다케이스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James Douglas 1st of Dalkeith родился в 1325 г. в местности далкит, мидлотян, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir John Douglas of Lothian было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes Graham - Heiress of Dalkeith, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Agnes de Dunbar около 1347 г. в местности далкит, мидлотян, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер в 1420 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 95 лет и был похоронен в местности далкит, мидлотян, Шотландия, Великобритания.
